skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Guides AI-assisted code reviews across security, performance, maintainability, tests, documentation, and issue priority.
skill.summary: Review code with structured security, performance, maintainability, and test coverage checks.
skill.author: Cain96

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many risky strings, but review found they are Markdown examples in a code review guide, not executable skill code. The main publishable concern is that the workflow recommends package-manager commands, which can run project-defined scripts if an agent has shell access. No prompt injection attempt or credential exfiltration intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Package Script Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with limited scope. The skill instructs automated review checks and lists package-manager commands such as lint, type checking, tests, audit, build, and analyze. In an assistant environment with shell access, those commands may execute project-defined scripts from an untrusted repository. The skill frontmatter only allows Read, Grep, and Glob, so this is a contextual risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is clearly present and could be risky when shell tools are available. Confidence is medium because the skill itself only declares read-oriented tools and contains no executable script file.

low_findings.title: Unsafe Code Examples Misclassified as Active Vulnerabilities
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The innerHTML assignment, SQL string interpolation, hardcoded API key, and process.env access are shown as educational examples of bad and good patterns. They are inside Markdown code blocks and are not active skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text labels these snippets as common vulnerabilities and immediately provides safer alternatives. There is no executable file or runtime path using these snippets.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The many backtick-related static findings are Markdown fences used to format TypeScript, Bash, and Markdown examples. They are not Ruby or shell backtick execution in a program.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited occurrence is in Markdown documentation or example code. No Ruby file, shell script, or executable backtick expression exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to public documentation resources for OWASP, clean code guidance, React, and TypeScript. The skill does not contain code that fetches or sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in a resources section as Markdown links. No network request API or exfiltration behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Unsupported
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The scanned file contains no cryptographic implementation, hashing function call, cipher selection, or password handling logic. The static hits appear to come from broad pattern matching against documentation text or filenames.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic code at the cited locations. The cited lines are skill metadata, output examples, checklist text, and review focus text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Commands in Review Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill suggests running project commands during review. This can be appropriate for trusted projects, but package scripts may execute arbitrary code in untrusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit, but the skill declares only read-oriented tools. The risk depends on whether the host agent grants shell execution outside the skill metadata.

content.user_title: Review Code for Security and Quality
content.value_statement: Manual code reviews can miss security, performance, and test coverage issues. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured checklist for consistent review feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code for common security issues such as injection, XSS, secrets, authorization gaps, and dependency risk.
Checks performance concerns including N+1 queries, inefficient algorithms, unnecessary re-renders, and missing caching.
Evaluates maintainability through complexity, function length, nesting, naming, duplication, and documentation checks.
Assesses test coverage expectations for critical code, business logic, utilities, and UI components.
Structures review output by severity so teams can prioritize fixes before merge.
Provides examples of common code smells and cleaner alternatives.
limitations: It is a review guide, not a standalone scanner or testing tool.
It does not include executable scripts or integrations with CI systems.
Automated command suggestions require a trusted repository and explicit tool permission.
Examples focus mainly on TypeScript, JavaScript, React, and common web application patterns.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review a pending change for security issues, regressions, missing tests, and maintainability concerns before merge.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Quality Check
use_cases.description: Check critical application areas for unsafe patterns, performance risk, and incomplete test coverage before release.

use_cases.title: Learning Review Standards
use_cases.description: Use the checklist and examples to learn what strong code review feedback should cover.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change for security, performance, maintainability, and tests. List the most important issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request as a merge gate. Separate blocking issues from suggestions and include file or line references when available.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code with extra focus on authentication, authorization, input validation, secret handling, and data exposure.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Architecture Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a larger change
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full review covering business logic correctness, architecture, performance, security, test quality, and documentation. Prioritize risks by severity.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds a new user search endpoint and React results list.
output: High: Validate and parameterize the search input before querying user records.
Medium: Add loading and empty states for the results list.
Medium: Add tests for empty input, long input, and unauthorized access.

output_examples.input: A service method loops through users and fetches posts one user at a time.
output: High: Replace repeated per-user queries with one joined or batched query.
Medium: Add a regression test that covers multiple users with posts.
Low: Document expected result ordering for callers.

output_examples.input: A large payment handler combines validation, pricing, persistence, and notifications.
output: Critical: Confirm idempotency before charging or retrying payment work.
High: Split validation, pricing, persistence, and notification steps into focused units.
Medium: Add tests for failed payment, retry, and duplicate request paths.

best_practices: Review security and correctness before style or naming feedback.
Ask for file and line references so feedback is actionable.
Treat package script execution as unsafe unless the repository is trusted.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a replacement for tests, security scanners, or CI checks.
Do not run project commands from untrusted repositories without explicit approval.
Do not report vague concerns without severity, impact, and a concrete fix.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews security, performance, maintainability, test coverage, documentation, and issue priority.

faq.question: Does it run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide and declares only read-oriented tools.

faq.question: Can it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure review feedback, but human judgment is still required.

faq.question: Which languages does it fit best?
faq.answer: The examples fit TypeScript, JavaScript, React, and common web application code.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Read-only review is safer. Running suggested package commands requires trust and explicit permission.

faq.question: Can it help with test coverage?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks coverage targets and suggests missing edge case, error path, and critical path tests.
